The Rubus affinis microgene serraticuspis is a species of bramble, a type of thorny shrub that produces edible fruits. This plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to support digestive health and reduce inflammation, likely due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins. The bioactive constituents of Rubus species typically include ellagic acid, salicylic acid, and other phenolic compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties.
